query: Current operational status of Iran Bushehr Unit 1 nuclear reactor in July 2026 - is it still generating power and connected to grid, or was it shut down? Latest IAEA, Rosatom, AEOI statements after July 8 2026 US strikes near Bushehr
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings on Bushehr Unit 1's operational status as of July 2026:

## Key Finding
**There is no authoritative confirmation of whether Bushehr Unit 1 is currently generating power/grid-connected or has been shut down** following the July 8–9, 2026 US strikes. The situation is genuinely ambiguous from open sources.

## What is established
- **The reactor itself has NOT been confirmed struck, damaged, or leaking radiation.**
- **Iran's position (AEOI-adjacent officials):** Strikes hit only the plant's *perimeter/premises* around noon on July 9; provincial authorities explicitly **denied** any direct attack on facilities and any radioactive release.
- **Unconfirmed escalation claim:** A Russian-linked outlet (Pravda Ukraine, ~July 11–12) alleged the US struck the **machine hall / turbine hall** "just a few meters from the reactor." This is **not independently verified** by IAEA, Rosatom, or Western sources — but if true, it would be the detail most relevant to power generation, since a turbine-hall hit could force grid disconnection even while the reactor core stays safely cooled.
- **IAEA (Grossi, July 10):** Said the agency was **not confirming any direct attack on the reactor** and continues to monitor closely.
- **US CENTCOM:** Reported ~90 military targets struck but did **not** specifically acknowledge Bushehr.

## Important context
- Rosatom conducted a **near-total staff evacuation** in late March–April 2026 (200–320 leaving; ~20 volunteers remaining), then announced (~July 6) staff would **return from mid-July** — right before the new strikes.
- An April 4 strike killed one plant employee; IAEA has consistently reported **no radiation increase** throughout 2026.
- Reference databases (IAEA PRIS, Global Energy Monitor) still list Unit 1 as "Operating," but these lag real-time conflict events.

The structured findings, data points, and full source list have been recorded.

sources.title: Iran says US projectiles strike perimeter of Bushehr nuclear plant
sources.snippet: The perimeter of Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant was struck by projectiles during US attacks on July 9, 2026; no immediate casualties reported.

sources.title: Iran Says U.S. Strikes Targeted Bushehr Nuclear Plant, Warns of Retaliation Against American Bases
sources.snippet: US struck perimeter of Bushehr plant and a railway bridge on July 9; Iran threatened retaliation against US bases.

sources.title: The U.S. carried out a missile and bomb strike on the machine hall of the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
sources.snippet: Claims US struck the machine hall (turbine hall) 'just a few meters from the reactor'; unverified by IAEA/Rosatom.

sources.title: Attacks on any nuclear plants, including Zaporozhye, Kursk, Bushehr unacceptable — Grossi
sources.snippet: July 10, 2026: Grossi, after meeting Rosatom CEO Likhachev, said IAEA not confirming a direct attack on Bushehr; continues monitoring.

sources.title: Iranian authorities deny reports of an attack on the Bushehr nuclear power plant and a radiation leak
sources.snippet: Bushehr province deputy governor denied a strike on plant facilities and denied any radioactive release/contamination.

sources.title: Iran alleges US strikes on Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
sources.snippet: Iran says strikes hit around noon July 9; US CENTCOM struck ~90 military targets but did not specifically acknowledge Bushehr; no clarity on damage.

sources.title: IAEA: Monitoring situation at Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant
sources.snippet: July 10, 2026: IAEA DG says agency closely monitoring Bushehr amid renewed US-Iran hostilities.

sources.title: Rosatom Prepares Final Staff Evacuation From Bushehr Nuclear Plant
sources.snippet: Rosatom to evacuate employees (200+ to Armenia; ~320 total Russians) while volunteers remain to keep plant operational.

sources.title: Russian staff to return to Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant from mid July, cites Rosatom
sources.snippet: Around July 6, Rosatom (via Interfax) said staff would return to Bushehr from mid-July to resume work.

sources.title: Projectile hits near Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant, killing one: IAEA
sources.snippet: April 4, 2026: one plant employee killed by projectile fragments; IAEA reported no radiation rise.

sources.title: Bushehr 1 - World Nuclear Association reactor database
sources.snippet: VVER V-446 PWR, net capacity 915 MWe; first grid connection Sept 3, 2011; commercial operation Sept 23, 2013.

sources.title: Bushehr nuclear power plant - Global Energy Monitor
sources.snippet: Lists Unit 1 status as 'Operating' (database may lag real-time conflict events).

sources.title: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Iran claimed area near plant attacked four times during 2026 war as of April 5, killing one employee; IAEA reported no radiation rise.

sources.title: Russia evacuates most staff from Bushehr nuclear plant (Rosatom/Likhachev)
sources.snippet: Likhachev said 108 workers leaving with ~20 remaining to monitor equipment amid strikes.

data_points.metric: Rosatom staff reported leaving Bushehr in early-April 2026 evacuation (upper estimate)
Rosatom volunteers reportedly remaining at Bushehr to monitor equipment
Plant employees killed in April 4, 2026 strike near Bushehr
Distance of March 2026 projectile strike from active reactor
US military targets struck in Iran in July 8-9 2026 operation (CENTCOM, Bushehr not specifically acknowledged)
